qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the compute nodes on the Béluga cluster that are equipped with GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: The Béluga cluster includes 172 compute nodes that are equipped with GPUs. Each of these nodes has 40 cores, 186G (or 191000M) of available memory, two Intel Gold 6148 Skylake @ 2.4 GHz CPUs, 1 x NVMe SSD 1.6T for storage, and four NVidia V100SXM2 GPUs, each with 16G memory, connected via NVLink.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor the performance of their jobs on the Béluga cluster, and why is this recommended?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can monitor their CPU and GPU past or current compute tasks in real-time through the Béluga portal at https://portail.beluga.calculquebec.ca/. This allows them to track the use of compute cores, memory, and GPUs. It is recommended to monitor jobs to maximize resource use and reduce waiting times in the queue by adjusting job resource requests if allocated resources are underutilized.
